creator: frederique@netground.nl
text:
I see these kind of 'garbled' logging on various servers, various versions and archs:

<<1110>10>iippffww::  65600 500A cAccecpetp tU DP U8D7.P2 5807..125404..61i4:p4f49w5:.0 69 :6865020.68043 .2A4c3 c6e.p82t01 .U28D4:P.5 23837 6.o.2ut52 01v2i:.5a13 4 4io.g6ubt0 :

<<<11101>110i0>a1> pi7fg6b9w0 :

<<111010> >0.8645.0203 6Ac.c2ep1t 2U:5D3P  8ou7t .v2i5a0. 1i4g4b.06

<<1101>1i0>pifpwf:w : 68006 80D0 eDneyn y UUDDPP  119944..113344..4411..1122::560877110  8877..225500..114444..1100::5533  iinn  vviiaa  iiggbb00

<<110>1710>.295:02.11 4i4n. 1v0i:a2 1i gibn0

Fix: 

Some have suggested to compile this into the kernel:

options         PRINTF_BUFR_SIZE=128

. but I'd rather keep running GENERIC kernels (freebsd-update)
How-To-Repeat: No idea honstly, also cannot find a common factor for this issue. But almost every admin has seen it I think. I've seen them as long as I can remember.
